headline: Malaysian Sign Language App Reaches 85,000 Deaf Users
context: Government-backed Malaysian Sign Language digital platform reached 85,000 registered users among the estimated 300,000 deaf community.

cards.big: Sign language platform reaches 85,000 of 300,000 deaf community members
cards.sub: App provides video relay interpretation for government service access

cards.big: Users can access real-time sign language interpreters for 12 government services
cards.sub: Average wait time for interpreter connection is 8 minutes during business hours

cards.big: Service operates 8am-6pm weekdays leaving evenings and weekends uncovered
cards.sub: Only 180 certified interpreters serve the entire national platform

cards.big: Expansion to healthcare interpretation planned for 2027 with RM12 million budget
cards.sub: Emergency services access through sign language not yet available

cards.big: Accessibility technology tests whether digital inclusion extends to disability communities
85,000 deaf users on Malaysian Sign Language app. Assistive technology works when designed with, not for, its users.
